6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
388	  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
389	  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
390	  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
391	  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
392	      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
393	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
394	    have hOver := Over.w f
395	    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
396	    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
397	      at hOver
398	    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
399	    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
401	  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
402	  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
403	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
404	      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
405	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
406	  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
407	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
408	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
409	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
410	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
411	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
412	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
413	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
414	  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
415	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
416	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
417	  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
418	  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
419	  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
420	  change ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
421	    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
422	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
423	  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
424	  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
425	  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
426	  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
427	  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
428	    have hwr := congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
429	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hwr
430	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
431	    -- Now LHS = g_f.hom (algebraMap kbar GmRing r), RHS = structureRingMap.hom r
432	    exact hwr.symm
433	  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
434	    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
435	    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]
436	
437	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
438	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
439	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
440	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
442	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
443	  intro u
444	  apply Units.ext
445	  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
446	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
447	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
448	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
449	  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
450	  -- underlying scheme morphism.
451	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
452	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
453	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
454	        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
455	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
456	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
457	  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
458	  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
459	  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
460	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
461	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
462	  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
463	  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
464	
465	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
466	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
467	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
468	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
469	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
470	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
471	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
472	  apply Units.ext
473	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
474	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
475	  rfl
476	
477	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
478	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
479	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
480	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
481	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
482	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
483	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
484	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
485	  homEquiv {T} :=
486	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
487	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
488	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
489	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
490	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
491	
492	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
493	
494	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
495	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
496	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
497	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
498	
499	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
500	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
501	`W = Gm`). -/
502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
506	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
507	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
508	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
509	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
510	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
511	
512	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
513	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
514	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
516	  η[Gm kbar]
517	
518	end AlgebraicGeometry
519	
520	end

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry
157	
158	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
159	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
160	acceptable for iter-165). -/
161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
210	        have := hp
211	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
212	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
213	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
214	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
215	        exact this
216	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
217	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
218	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
219	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
220	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
221	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
222	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
223	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
224	        by_contra h
225	        push Not at h
226	        apply hd_ne
227	        ext k
228	        fin_cases k
229	        · simpa using h.1
230	        · simpa using h.2
231	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
232	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
233	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
234	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
235	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
236	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
237	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
238	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
239	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
240	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
241	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
242	
243	end AlgebraicGeometry
244	
245	end

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61	
62	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
64	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
65	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
66	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
67	
68	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
69	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
70	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
71	
72	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
73	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
80	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
81	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
82	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
83	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
84	
85	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
86	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
90	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
91	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
92	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
93	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
94	
95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>
104	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
105	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
107	
108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
143	
144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,

306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from

311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl

1582	  Let $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ be the scaling action

1590	\begin{proof}
1591	  \uses{def:gaTranslationP1, def:p1bar_zero, def:gm_one}
1592	  Chart-level. On the affine chart $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (coordinate $x$), the
1593	  morphism $\sigma_\times$ is the polynomial map $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda \cdot x$. Restricting
1594	  to the $\mathbb G_m$-section $x = 0$ gives the constant ring map
1595	  $\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \to \bar k$, $\lambda \mapsto 0$; equivalently the scheme morphism factors
1596	  through the closed point $0 \in \mathbb P^1$. The agreement with the other chart $u = 1/x$ near
1597	  $\infty$ is automatic (the section $x = 0$ does not meet that chart). The named equation is the
1598	  Lean encoding of this fact, with $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m}$ supplying the
1599	  $\Spec \bar k$-factor and $\mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m}$ the parameter $\lambda$.

1602	\begin{lemma}
1603	[$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$: a homomorphism from the additive group into an abelian variety is trivial]
1604	  \label{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1605	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1606	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
1607	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
1608	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
1609	  % NOTE (iter-164): the verbatim Milne Prop 3.9 statement+proof quotes are the prior-session-verified
1610	  % copies also carried on \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} below. This host lacks a PDF renderer
1611	  % (pdftoppm) so the quotes were NOT re-rendered from references/abelian-varieties.pdf this session;
1612	  % they are reproduced from the verified in-tree copy. See the writer report.
1613	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1614	
1615	  \textbf{Off the genus-$0$ critical path (iter-164).} This $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$
1616	  lemma is \emph{superseded by the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut}
1617	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}), which closes the
1618	  base case without it. It is retained only as the classical Milne Proposition~3.9 ($\mathbb
1619	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility) argument feeding the alternative route
1620	  \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}; no block on the genus-$0$ critical path depends on it.
1621	
1622	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$, and let
1623	  $\varphi \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ be a homomorphism of group objects (so $\varphi(0) = 0_A$ and
1624	  $\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)$). Then $\varphi$ is the trivial homomorphism: it
1625	  factors through the terminal object as the constant morphism at the identity,
1626	  $\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1627	
1628	  \medskip
1629	  \noindent\textbf{Equivalent completeness phrasing.} Equivalently: a homomorphism from an
1630	  \emph{affine connected} group object into a \emph{complete} group object is trivial. That is the
1631	  abstract content; the proof below realises it characteristic-free.
1632	\end{lemma}
1633	
1634	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
1635	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
1636	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
1637	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
1638	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
1639	\begin{proof}
1640	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
1641	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1642	  Write $A$ additively (an abelian variety is commutative). We give the conceptual
1643	  \emph{completeness} proof as the abstract argument, then record Milne's elementary $\mathbb
1644	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility, which applies whenever $\varphi$ extends to a morphism on
1645	  $\mathbb P^1$ (as it does in the genus-$0$ application).
1646	
1647	  \textbf{Completeness proof.} The image $\varphi(\mathbb G_a)$ is a connected subgroup of $A$
1648	  (image of the connected $\mathbb G_a$ under a homomorphism). As the image of a homomorphism of
1649	  algebraic groups it is a \emph{closed} subgroup of $A$, hence \emph{complete} (closed in the

1880	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} uses). Hence $f$ is constant, with value $f(1)$; in the normalised
1881	  picture $f(0) = 0_A$, so the constant is $0_A$ (the original $f(0)$ before translation).
1882	
1883	  \medskip
1884	  \noindent\emph{Multi-factor remark.} For the general unirational statement of Proposition~3.10,
1885	  one writes $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)$ via the additive decomposition
1886	  (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and applies this base case to each $\beta_i \colon
1887	  \mathbb P^1 \to A$; we need only the $d = 1$ case here. The Milne-faithful $\mathbb
1888	  G_a$-additive route (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}, via $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$,
1889	  \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}) reaches the same conclusion and is retained as an alternative,

1936	\section{A genus-$0$ curve over $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$}
1937	
1938	\begin{proposition}
1939	
1940	\leanok
1941	[Genus-$0$ curve $\cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$]
1942	  \label{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1943	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1944	  \uses{def:genus}
1945	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne, Algebraic Geometry], Example IV.1.3.5, Ch.~IV \S1 (Riemann--Roch), doc p.~297 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314)
1946	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is rational if it is birational to
1947	  % $\mathbf P^1$ (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in this chapter are complete and nonsingular by
1948	  % definition, a curve $X$ is rational if and only if $X \cong \mathbf P^1$ (I, 6.12). Now
1949	  % using (1.3) we can show that $X$ is rational if and only if $g = 0$. We already know that
1950	  % $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely suppose given a curve $X$ of genus 0.
1951	  % Let $P, Q$ be two distinct points of $X$ and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor
1952	  % $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg(K - D) = -2$, using (1.3.3) above, we have $l(K - D) = 0$, and so
1953	  % we find $l(D) = 1$. But $D$ is a divisor of degree 0, so by (1.2) we have $D \sim 0$, in
1954	  % other words $P \sim Q$. But this implies $X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
1955	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, Algebraic Geometry, Example~IV.1.3.5.}
1956	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $C$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
1957	  over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
1958	\end{proposition}
1959	
1960	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the statement block above
1961	% (Hartshorne states the classification and its proof inside the single Example; the proof is
1962	% the second half beginning ``We already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ \ldots'').
1963	\begin{proof}
1964	  \uses{def:genus}
1965	  \emph{Proof sketch (Riemann--Roch level; the formalisation of this step is itself a
1966	  sub-build --- Mathlib has no Riemann--Roch for curves).}
1967	
1968	  Over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$ every closed point is $\bar k$-rational, so $C$
1969	  has at least two distinct $\bar k$-points $P \neq Q$ (a smooth proper geometrically
1970	  irreducible curve over $\bar k$ is infinite). With $\genus(C) = \dim_{\bar k}
1971	  H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0$, apply the Riemann--Roch theorem to the degree-$0$ divisor
1972	  $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg(K - D) = 2g - 2 - \deg D = -2 < 0$ one has $l(K - D) = 0$, so
1973	  Riemann--Roch $l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g$ gives $l(D) = 0 + 1 - 0 = 1$. Thus the
1974	  degree-$0$ divisor $D$ is linearly equivalent to an effective divisor, forcing $D \sim 0$,
1975	  i.e.\ $P \sim Q$. A curve possessing two distinct linearly equivalent points is rational
1976	  (Hartshorne II.6.10.1: $P \sim Q$ for $P \neq Q$ yields a degree-$1$ map to $\mathbb P^1$),
1977	  and a complete nonsingular rational curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$ (Hartshorne I.6.12).
1978	  Hence $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
1979	\end{proof}

321	/-- Given a family of homogeneous elements `f` of positive degree that spans the irrelevant ideal,
322	`Spec (A_f)₀ ⟶ Proj A` forms an affine open cover of `Proj A`. -/
323	noncomputable
324	def affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan {ι : Type*} (f : ι → A) {m : ι → ℕ}
325	    (f_deg : ∀ i, f i ∈ 𝒜 (m i)) (hm : ∀ i, 0 < m i)
326	    (hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal ≤ Ideal.span (Set.range f)) :
327	    (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover where
328	  I₀ := ι
329	  X i := .of (Away 𝒜 (f i))
330	  f i := awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)
331	  idx x := (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose
332	  covers x := by
333	    change x ∈ (awayι 𝒜 _ _ _).opensRange
334	    rw [opensRange_awayι]
335	    exact (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose_spec
336	
337	/-- `Proj A` is covered by `Spec (A_f)₀` for all homogeneous elements of positive degree. -/
338	@[simps! f] noncomputable
339	def affineOpenCover : (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover :=
340	  affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜
341	    (ι := Σ i : PNat, 𝒜 i) (m := fun i ↦ i.1) (fun i ↦ i.2) (fun i ↦ i.2.2) (fun i ↦ i.1.2) <| by
342	  classical
343	  intro z hz
344	  rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose 𝒜 z]
345	  refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun c hc ↦ if hc0 : c = 0 then ?_ else
346	    Ideal.subset_span ⟨⟨⟨c, Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hc0⟩, _⟩, rfl⟩
347	  convert Ideal.zero_mem _
348	  subst hc0
349	  exact hz

371	def toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections (H : f t = x) (h0d : 0 < d) (hd : t ∈ 𝒜 d) :
372	    (X.basicOpen x).toScheme ⟶ basicOpen 𝒜 t := by

377	  refine IsLocalization.map (M := .powers t) (T := .powers x) _ f ?_
378	  · rw [← Submonoid.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submonoid.map_powers]
379	    simp [H]

968	        change _ = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
969	        ext

have : IsLocalization.Away (1 - e) (↑R ⧸ Ideal.span {e}) :=
        IsLocalization.away_of_isIdempotentElem he.one_sub (by simp) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization (1 - e)
    have H : Set.range (Spec.map φ) = Set.range (Spec.map f) :=
      ((range_comap_of_surjective _ _
        Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective).trans (by simp)).trans he'.symm
    let i : S ≅ .of _ := (Scheme.Spec.preimageIso
      (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map f) H)).unop
    have hi : Function.Injective i.inv.hom := (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso i.inv).1
--
    have : IsLocalization.Away (1 - e) S :=
      IsLocalization.away_of_isIdempotentElem he.one_sub (by simpa using he') hf
    exact IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization (1 - e)

/-- **Iter-182 Lane E primary helper:** the composed morphism
`(lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left ≫ gmScalingP1.left : Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left` is an
open immersion.

**Mathematical content.** Per `analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 4: the
composition realises the canonical inclusion `λ ↦ [λ : 1]` of `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` into
`ℙ¹` as the chart-1 of the `gmScalingP1_cover`. The factorisation chain is

```
Gm.left = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]
   ↪ Spec k̄[u]               (`Spec.map (algebraMap k̄[u] k̄[u, u⁻¹])`, an open immersion
                                via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`)
   ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))     (via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.symm`, an iso)
   ↪ ℙ¹.left                  (via `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1)`, an open immersion).
```

Composing the three open immersions gives an open immersion `Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`.

**Status (iter-182 honest direct sorry).** The factorisation chain requires unfolding
`gmScalingP1.left` to its `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` form, building the chart-1
section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1` via `pullback.lift` (using the chart-1 factorisation
of `onePt.left` through `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1)` — itself the underlying-scheme content of
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` applied to `onePt`'s `pointOfVec` realisation),
applying `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` to identify `s ≫ (cover).f 1 ≫ gmScalingP1.left`
with `s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1`, then composing through the three open immersions of
`gmScalingP1_chart 1`'s definition (the chart-iso, the ring-map `Spec.map`, and
`Proj.awayι`). The latter two ARE open immersions individually; the first (the iso
`gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso 1`) trivially is. The substantive missing piece is the
explicit chart-1 factorisation of `onePt.left`, which composes the `pointOfVec`'s
`fromOfGlobalSections` realisation with the standard `awayι` factorisation; this
~30-60 LOC chase is deferred to iter-183+. -/
private lemma iotaGm_isOpenImmersion [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
    IsOpenImmersion ((lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar)
        (𝟙 (Gm kbar))).left ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar).left) := by
  -- Strategy (per `analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 4):
  --   (a) `(lift _ _).left = pullback.lift (Gm.hom ≫ onePt.left) (𝟙 Gm.left) _` via
  --       `Over.lift_left`; combined with the unfolding `(gmScalingP1).left =
  --       (cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart _` from the `Over.homMk` definition.
  --   (b) Extract `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))` such that
  --       `onePt.left = r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1) _ _` via
  --       `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` chased through `pointOfVec`.
  --   (c) Build the section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1 = pullback q (awayι (X 1))` via
  --       `pullback.lift (lift_left form) (Gm.hom ≫ r_1) compatibility`.
  --   (d) `s ≫ (cover).f 1 = (lift _ _).left` by `pullback.lift_fst` (this matches
  --       the over-structure projector).
  --   (e) `s ≫ (cover).f 1 ≫ gmScalingP1.left = s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1` by
  --       `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms`. Hence
  --       `(lift _ _).left ≫ gmScalingP1.left = s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1`.
  --   (f) `gmScalingP1_chart 1 = iso.hom ≫ Spec.map ring_map ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` (its
  --       definition). After composing with `s`, the chain
  --         `Gm.left → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1) ⊗ GmRing) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) → ℙ¹.left`
  --       is three open immersions: localization-Spec.map + iso + `Proj.awayι`.
  -- The (b)+(f) chart-side chases are the substantive content — both project-specific
  -- (no single Mathlib lemma exposes them) and ~30-60 LOC each. Deferred to iter-183+.
  --
  -- Iter-182 structural progress: drive steps (a) by `Over.lift_left` + the
  -- `Over.homMk`-definitional unfolding of `gmScalingP1.left` to its glued form, then

  change IsOpenImmersion (pullback.lift _ _ _ ≫
    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
      (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar))

input.new_string:
/-- **Sub-task (b) helper (iter-183 Lane E):** the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` factors
through the chart-1 affine open `D₊(X 1) ⊆ ℙ¹` via `Proj.awayι (X 1)`.

**Mathematical content.** `onePt.left = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 (evalIntoGlobal v) _`
with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`. Since `evalIntoGlobal v (X 1) = (ΓSpecIso k̄).inv 1 = 1` is a unit in
`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`, the preimage of `D₊(X 1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` under `onePt.left` is
`(Spec k̄).basicOpen 1 = ⊤`. Hence by `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` the
restriction of `onePt.left` to `D₊(X 1)` is the entire morphism (modulo the trivial
`(Spec k̄)|_⊤ ≅ Spec k̄` identification), so `onePt.left = r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` for
the unique `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))` corresponding to the ring map
`Away 𝒜 (X 1) →+* k̄`, `[a/X_1^n] ↦ eval_v(a)`.

The ring map is concretely
`(MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Unit => (1:kbar))).comp (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso 1).toRingHom`,
i.e. through the chart-ring iso send `[X_0/X_1] ↦ 1` and constants `[r] ↦ r`.

**Status (iter-183 honest direct sorry, Tier-3).** The substantive `r_1 ≫ awayι X_1 =
onePt.left` equation is the chart-1 factorisation chase through
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` and the `basicOpenIsoSpec` identification —
project-specific and ~30-50 LOC. iter-184+ closure target. -/
private lemma iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ∃ r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
        Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
          ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
            Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))),
      r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

composition realises the canonical inclusion `λ ↦ [λ : 1]` of `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` into
`ℙ¹` as the chart-1 of the `gmScalingP1_cover`. The factorisation chain is

```
Gm.left = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]
   ↪ Spec k̄[u]               (`Spec.map (algebraMap k̄[u] k̄[u, u⁻¹])`, an open immersion
                                via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`)
   ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))     (via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.symm`, an iso)
   ↪ ℙ¹.left                  (via `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1)`, an open immersion).
```

Composing the three open immersions gives an open immersion `Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`.
