96	open CategoryTheory Limits
97	
98	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
99	
100	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
101	
102	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
103	curve `C / k̄`.**
104	
105	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
106	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
107	alternating sum
108	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
109	expression
110	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
111	
112	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
113	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
114	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
115	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
116	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
117	
118	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
119	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
120	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
121	
122	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
123	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
124	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
125	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
126	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
127	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
128	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
129	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
130	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
131	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
132	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
133	
134	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (placeholder)
135	
136	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
137	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of
138	closed points are glued into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. To keep the
139	iter-174 skeleton's pin signatures substantive, we expose `𝒪_C(D)` as a
140	typed-`sorry` `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf-on-`C`. The iter-175+ closure of
141	`RR.3` will replace the body with the construction; downstream pins (the
142	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) consume
143	this placeholder only through its `H⁰` and `H¹`, so their signatures
144	encode arithmetic identities on finiteness-of-cohomology outputs and are
145	not vacuous. -/
146	
147	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
148	
149	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
151	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
152	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
153	
154	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper
155	curve `C / k̄`** (placeholder for `RR.3`).
156	
157	For a Weil divisor `D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` on a smooth proper geometrically
158	irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` is the
159	locally-free `𝒪_C`-module of rank one cut out (locally near each `Pᵢ`) by
160	the `(−nᵢ)`-th power of the local uniformiser at `Pᵢ`. Hartshorne II §6
161	Proposition 6.13 packages this construction; the project-side `RR.3`
162	chapter (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, to be added) will produce the carrier as
163	a `Sheaf` of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules on `C.left`.
164	
165	In the iter-174 skeleton this is a typed-`sorry`. The body in `RR.3` will
166	be the invertible-sheaf glue of the locally principal ideals of the
167	support of `D`. -/
168	noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
169	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
170	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
174	
175	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
176	`C / k̄`.**
177	
178	By definition,
179	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
180	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
181	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
182	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
183	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
184	of `genus C`.
185	
186	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
187	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
188	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
191	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
192	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
193	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
194	
195	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
196	
197	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
198	
199	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
200	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
201	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
202	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
203	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
204	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
205	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
206	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
207	body.
208	
209	**Iter-181 Lane H factoring.** Both Hartshorne IV.1.3 inputs (the base
210	case `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` and the inductive step `χ(𝒪_C(D + Y)) = χ(𝒪_C(D))
211	+ n`) intrinsically reference the body of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf`,
212	which is still a typed-`sorry` placeholder waiting on the sibling
213	chapter `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). We therefore factor the proof
214	into two named substantive helper lemmas (3-tier disclosure: **honest
215	named-sorry helpers** — each helper has a substantive type encoding a
216	nontrivial mathematical claim about the `sheafOf` line bundle whose
217	closure is downstream of the `RR.3` body):
218	
219	1. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` — base case `χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 − g`,
220	   which on closure of `RR.3` reduces to `sheafOf 0 = toModuleKSheaf C`
221	   plus the Hartshorne I.3.4 input `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` plus the
222	   definition of the genus.
223	2. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` — inductive step
224	   `χ(sheafOf (Finsupp.single Y n + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + n` for any
225	   `Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor` and `n : ℤ`. On closure of `RR.3` this
226	   reduces to the Hartshorne IV.1.3 SES additivity argument iterated
227	   `|n|` times (with sign for `n < 0`).
228	
229	The main theorem then closes by induction on the `Finsupp`-structure of
230	`D : C.left.WeilDivisor` (via `Finsupp.induction`), using the two
231	helpers and the additivity of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree`. -/
232	
233	/-- **Base case of the χ-identity** (Lane H helper 1/2): on a smooth
234	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the Euler characteristic
235	of the structure-sheaf-side line bundle `sheafOf 0` is `1 − g(C)`. -/
236	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero
237	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
238	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
239	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
240	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left] :
241	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
242	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor))
243	      = 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
244	  -- On closure of `RR.3`, `sheafOf 0 ≅ toModuleKSheaf C` (the structure
245	  -- sheaf), so `χ(sheafOf 0) = dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) - dim H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
246	  -- Hartshorne I.3.4 gives `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` (a proper geometrically
247	  -- irreducible variety over an algebraically closed field has
248	  -- `H⁰(𝒪) = k`); the definition of `genus` gives
249	  -- `dim H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = g(C)`. Substituting yields `1 - g`. The full
250	  -- closure waits on `RR.3` supplying a body for `sheafOf`.
251	  sorry
252	
253	/-- **Inductive step of the χ-identity** (Lane H helper 2/2): on a smooth
254	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the Euler characteristic
255	of `sheafOf` transports across the elementary modification
256	`D ↦ Finsupp.single Y n + D` with arithmetic increment `n`. The argument
257	`D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than `Scheme.WeilDivisor`
258	so that the `Finsupp.single Y n + D` term elaborates cleanly; the result
259	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
260	ℤ)`. -/
261	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add
262	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
263	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
264	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
265	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
266	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
267	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
268	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y n + D))
269	      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
270	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + n := by
271	  -- For `n ≥ 0`, iterate the Hartshorne IV.1.3 closed-point SES
272	  --   `0 → 𝒪_C(D + k·Y) → 𝒪_C(D + (k+1)·Y) → k(Y) → 0`
273	  -- `n` times, using additivity of χ on SES and `χ(k(Y)) = 1`.
274	  -- For `n < 0` apply the same argument to `-n` and use additivity
275	  -- of `sheafOf` under the inverse line bundle. The full closure waits
276	  -- on `RR.3` supplying a body for `sheafOf` and the project-side
277	  -- helper for SES additivity of χ (Mathlib gap: no
278	  -- `CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar_additive` under that name —
279	  -- see blueprint chapter §4 Lean reference note).
280	  sorry
281	
282	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
283	of genus `g`.**
284	
285	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
286	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
287	
288	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
289	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
290	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
291	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
292	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
293	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
294	
295	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
296	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295).
297	
298	**Iter-181 Lane H status** — the main theorem is closed by induction on
299	the `Finsupp` structure of `D`, consuming the two `sheafOf`-side helper
300	sorries `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` (base case) and
301	`eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` (inductive step). The body
302	itself is honest; its `sorryAx`-transitivity is the inevitable
303	consequence of `sheafOf` still being a typed sorry waiting on `RR.3`. -/
304	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
305	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
306	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
307	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
308	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
309	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
310	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
311	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
312	  -- Unfold `WeilDivisor` to expose the underlying `Finsupp` structure so
313	  -- `Finsupp.induction` applies directly.
314	  unfold Scheme.WeilDivisor at D
315	  induction D using Finsupp.induction with
316	  | zero =>
317	    -- D = 0: χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 - g by helper 1, and degree 0 = 0.
318	    exact (Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero C).trans
319	      (by simp [Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree])
320	  | single_add Y n D' _hY _hn hD' =>
321	    -- D = single Y n + D': use helper 2 then the inductive hypothesis.
322	    rw [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add, hD']
323	    -- Goal: (degree D' + 1 - g) + n = degree (single Y n + D') + 1 - g.
324	    have hdeg : Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree
325	        ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.WeilDivisor))
326	        = n + Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D' := by
327	      change ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)).sum
328	          (fun _ z => z) = _
329	      classical
330	      rw [Finsupp.sum_add_index (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ _ _ => rfl)]
331	      simp [Finsupp.sum_single_index]
332	      rfl
333	    linarith
334	
335	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
336	
337	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
338	
339	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
340	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
341	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
342	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
343	
344	The proof specialises the χ-identity
345	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
346	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
347	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
348	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
349	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
350	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
351	
352	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
353	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
354	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
355	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
356	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
357	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
358	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
359	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
360	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
361	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
362	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
363	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
364	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 := by
365	  have h := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus C D
366	  simp only [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic, _hg, _hH1,
367	    Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at h
368	  exact h
369	
370	end AlgebraicGeometry
371

3	\chapter{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)}
4	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}
5	
6	% SOURCE: Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, IV \S1, pp.~294--297
7	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages
8	% 311--314). The verbatim quotes pasted in the declaration blocks below
9	% were copied character-by-character from the rendered page images of
10	% the scanned PDF (no text layer is present).
11	
12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	
14	This chapter is the second of four sub-build chapters \texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4}
15	that close the project's headline RR bridge
16	\[
17	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
18	  \,\colon\,
19	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C / \bar k$ with
20	  $g(C) = 0$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.}
21	\]
22	The bridge consumed by
23	\Cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} of
24	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex} is the Hartshorne~IV.1.3.5 chain:
25	the Weil-divisor group $\Div(C)$ and the degree map $\deg \colon \Div(C)
26	\to \mathbb Z$ (\texttt{RR.1}, \Cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}); the
27	Riemann--Roch dimension formula in genus~$0$
28	\[
29	  \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0
30	\]
31	(this chapter, \texttt{RR.2}); the global-sections identification
32	$\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2$ (\texttt{RR.3}); the
33	``rational curve $\Rightarrow \cong \mathbb P^1$'' classification via
34	\texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} (\texttt{RR.4}).
35	
36	% NOTE (Serre duality vs. direct $\chi$ — iter-173 sub-phase choice).
37	% This chapter proves the RR formula via the \emph{direct $\chi$
38	% inductive proof} (Hartshorne IV.1.3 step ``Next, let $D$ be any
39	% divisor\ldots'', p.~296), \emph{not} via Serre duality. The reason is
40	% strictly Mathlib-side: Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships no
41	% dualizing sheaf $\omega_X$ on a scheme and no Serre duality theorem
42	% $H^i(X, \mathcal F) \cong H^{n-i}(X, \omega_X \otimes \mathcal F^\vee)^*$;
43	% routing through that machinery would force a multi-iter sub-build
44	% strictly heavier than the genus-$0$ critical path requires. The
45	% direct $\chi$ proof depends only on additivity of the Euler
46	% characteristic on a short exact sequence of coherent sheaves (a
47	% standard preadditive-category fact), the genus base case
48	% $\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g$, and the closed-point skyscraper
49	% identity $\chi(k(P)) = 1$.
50	
51	% NOTE (Genus-$0$ carve-out vs. general $g$ — iter-173 sub-phase
52	% choice). The chapter restricts the headline theorem
53	% \Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} to the genus-$0$ specialisation
54	% $\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1$ for $\deg(D) \geq 0$. The general-$g$
55	% statement $\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g$ is downstream of
56	% \texttt{RR.2} (it requires the canonical divisor $K$ and Serre
57	% duality / the $K - D$ pairing) and is \emph{not} needed for the
58	% genus-$0$ critical path: \texttt{RR.3} consumes only
59	% \Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}, and \texttt{RR.4} consumes
60	% \texttt{RR.3}. The intermediate $\chi$-identity
61	% \Cref{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus} is stated and
62	% sketched for general $g$ (the inductive argument is genus-uniform);
63	% its full closure on a general-$g$ curve waits for the project's
64	% subsequent Riemann--Roch chapter, but the proof body in
65	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean} pins the
66	% \emph{statement} for general $g$ and may close the body initially
67	% under a $g = 0$ hypothesis.
68	
69	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, $\bar k$ is an
70	algebraically closed field and $C$ is a smooth proper geometrically
71	irreducible curve over $\bar k$. Concretely, the Lean signatures
72	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean}) carry the same
73	typeclass package as the rest of the project's curve API:
74	\begin{quote}
75	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
76	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
77	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
78	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]}
79	\end{quote}
80	The genus $g(C)$ is the one defined in \Cref{def:genus} of
81	\texttt{Genus.tex}, namely $g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$
82	via the project's $\Module\bar k$-flavoured sheaf cohomology
83	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}).
84	
85	\section{The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve}
86	
87	\begin{definition}
88	
89	
90	\leanok
91	[Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve]
92	  \label{def:eulerChar_curve}
93	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic}
94	  \uses{def:genus}
95	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, p.~295 (definition of $\chi(\mathcal F)$
96	  % inside the proof of Theorem~1.3) (read from
97	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
98	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any $D$,
99	  % $\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g$,
100	  % where for any coherent sheaf $\mathscr F$ on $X$, $\chi(\mathscr F)$
101	  % is the Euler characteristic
102	  % $\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)$."
103	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~295, the formula displayed
104	  inside the proof of Theorem~1.3.}
105	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper curve over $\bar k$ and let $\mathcal F$
106	  be a coherent sheaf on $C$. The \emph{Euler characteristic} of
107	  $\mathcal F$ is the integer
108	  \[
109	    \chi(\mathcal F) \;:=\;
110	    \sum_{i \geq 0} (-1)^i \,\dim_{\bar k} H^i(C, \mathcal F)
111	    \;=\;
112	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal F)
113	    \,-\,
114	    \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal F).
115	  \]
116	  The second equality is a curve-specific simplification: because
117	  $\dim C = 1$, Grothendieck vanishing (Hartshorne~III.2.7) gives
118	  $H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0$ for $i \geq 2$, so the alternating sum has
119	  only two terms. Coherence of $\mathcal F$ on the proper $\bar
120	  k$-scheme $C$ guarantees that both $H^0$ and $H^1$ are
121	  finite-dimensional $\bar k$-vector spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology
122	  finiteness theorem, the same finiteness backing
123	  \Cref{def:genus}), so the two terms are honest natural numbers and
124	  the difference is a well-defined integer.
125	
126	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
127	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic} takes a coherent
128	  sheaf $\mathcal F$ on $C$ and returns an integer; the natural input
129	  shape is the project's $\Module \bar k$-valued sheaf-cohomology
130	  wrapper $\HModule \bar k$ (\Cref{def:Scheme_HModule}) applied to a
131	  $\Module \bar k$-flavoured carrier built from $\mathcal F$, with
132	  $\dim_{\bar k}$ extracted via $\Module.\mathtt{finrank}$. The
133	  declaration is \texttt{noncomputable} (inherited from
134	  $\Module.\mathtt{finrank}$ and from the $\Ext$-based cohomology
135	  construction).
136	\end{definition}
137	
138	\section{The $\ell$-invariant of a Weil divisor}
139	
140	\begin{definition}
141	
142	
143	\leanok
144	[$\ell$-invariant of a Weil divisor]
145	  \label{def:l_invariant}
146	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l}
147	  \uses{def:codim1_cycles, def:eulerChar_curve}
148	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, p.~295 (definition of $l(D)$ and of the
149	  % "dimension of $|D|$") (read from
150	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
151	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We denote $\dim_k H^0(X, \mathscr L(D))$ by $l(D)$,
152	  % so that the \emph{dimension of $|D|$} is $l(D) - 1$. The number
153	  % $l(D)$ is finite by (II, 5.19) or (III, 5.2)."
154	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~295 (definition of $l(D)$).}
155	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper curve over $\bar k$ and let
156	  $D \in \Div(C)$ be a Weil divisor (\Cref{def:codim1_cycles}). The
157	  \emph{$\ell$-invariant of $D$} is the integer
158	  \[
159	    \ell(D) \;:=\; \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
160	    \;\in\; \mathbb N,
161	  \]
162	  the $\bar k$-dimension of the space of global sections of the
163	  invertible sheaf $\mathcal O_C(D)$ associated to $D$. Finiteness of
164	  $\ell(D)$ is a consequence of the coherent-cohomology finiteness
165	  theorem on a proper $\bar k$-scheme (Hartshorne~II.5.19 or
166	  III.5.2). The classical \emph{complete linear system $|D|$} has
167	  $\bar k$-dimension $\ell(D) - 1$ as a projective space, but the
168	  chapter never uses the projective-space interpretation; $\ell(D)$ is
169	  the only quantity it needs.
170	
171	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
172	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l} accepts a divisor
173	  $D : C.\mathtt{WeilDivisor}$ (\Cref{def:codim1_cycles}) and returns
174	  $\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\;\bar k\;(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D)))$,
175	  computed through the same $\Module \bar k$-valued sheaf-cohomology
176	  pipeline as \Cref{def:eulerChar_curve}. The associated invertible
177	  sheaf $\mathcal O_C(D)$ is the line bundle of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
178	  chapter, to be added); the present chapter's prose and Lean
179	  signature reference $\mathcal O_C(D)$ by name and consume only its
180	  $H^0$ functor. The declaration is \texttt{noncomputable}.
181	\end{definition}
182	
183	\section{The $\chi$ identity: $\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g$}
184	
185	\begin{theorem}
186	
187	
188	\leanok
189	[Euler-characteristic identity for $\mathcal O_C(D)$]
190	  \label{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus}
191	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus}
192	  \uses{def:eulerChar_curve, def:divisor_degree, def:genus,
193	        thm:divisor_degree_hom}
194	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Theorem~1.3 (the reduction inside the
195	  % proof: ``Thus we have to show that for any $D$, $\chi(\mathscr L(D))
196	  % = \deg D + 1 - g$'') (read from
197	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
198	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Theorem 1.3 (Riemann--Roch). \emph{Let $D$ be a
199	  % divisor on a curve $X$ of genus $g$. Then}
200	  % $l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g$."
201	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any $D$,
202	  % $\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g$,
203	  % where for any coherent sheaf $\mathscr F$ on $X$, $\chi(\mathscr F)$
204	  % is the Euler characteristic
205	  % $\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)$."
206	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Theorem~1.3, p.~295.}
207	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
208	  $\bar k$ of genus $g = g(C)$, and let $D \in \Div(C)$. Then
209	  \[
210	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g.
211	  \]
212	\end{theorem}
213	
214	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "First we consider the case $D = 0$. Then our
215	% formula says
216	% $\dim H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = 1 - g$,
217	% because $H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) = k$ for any projective variety
218	% (I, 3.4), and $\dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = g$ by (1.1).
219	% Next, let $D$ be any divisor, and let $P$ be any point. We will show
220	% that the formula is true for $D$ if and only if it is true for
221	% $D + P$. Since any divisor can be reached from $0$ in a finite number
222	% of steps by adding or subtracting a point each time, this will show
223	% the result holds for all $D$.
224	% We consider $P$ as a closed subscheme of $X$. Its structure sheaf is
225	% the skyscraper sheaf $k$ sitting at the point $P$, which we denote by
226	% $k(P)$, and its ideal sheaf is $\mathscr L(-P)$ by (II, 6.18).
227	% Therefore we have an exact sequence
228	% $0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0$.
229	% Tensoring with $\mathscr L(D + P)$ we get
230	% $0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0$,
231	% (since $\mathscr L(P)$ is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by it
232	% does not affect the sheaf $k(P)$). Now the Euler characteristic is
233	% additive on short exact sequences (III, Ex. 5.1), and $\chi(k(P))
234	% = 1$, so we have
235	% $\chi(\mathscr L(D + P)) = \chi(\mathscr L(D)) + 1$.
236	% On the other hand, $\deg D + P = \deg D + 1$, so our formula is true
237	% for $D$ if and only if it is true for $D + P$, as required."
238	\begin{proof}
239	  \uses{def:eulerChar_curve, def:divisor_degree, def:genus,
240	        thm:divisor_degree_hom, def:divisor_closed_point}
241	  The proof is by induction on the structure of $\Div(C)$ as the free
242	  abelian group on closed points (\Cref{def:codim1_cycles},
243	  \Cref{def:divisor_closed_point}). Both sides of the asserted
244	  equation are additive in $D$ (the right-hand side by linearity of
245	  $\deg$, \Cref{thm:divisor_degree_hom}; the left-hand side by the
246	  inductive step proved below), so it suffices to verify the formula
247	  for $D = 0$ and to show that it transports across the elementary
248	  modification $D \mapsto D + [P]$ at any closed point $P \in C$.
249	
250	  \emph{Base case $D = 0$.} The line bundle $\mathcal O_C(0)$ is the
251	  structure sheaf $\mathcal O_C$, and the formula
252	  $\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g$ reads
253	  \[
254	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
255	    - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)
256	    \;=\; 1 - g.
257	  \]
258	  The second term is the definition of the genus,
259	  $\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = g$ (\Cref{def:genus}). The
260	  first term is $\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = 1$: $C$ is a
261	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over the algebraically
262	  closed field $\bar k$, so its connected components agree with its
263	  geometrically connected components, and $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
264	  \cong \bar k$ as $\bar k$-vector spaces (the Hartshorne~I.3.4
265	  statement that ``$H^0(X, \mathcal O_X) = k$ for any projective
266	  variety'' over an algebraically closed field; the Mathlib home is
267	  the project's $H^0$-bridge in
268	  \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex}). Substituting these
269	  two values gives $1 - g = 1 - g$, as required.
270	
271	  \emph{Inductive step (Hartshorne's ``$D \leftrightsquigarrow D + P$''
272	  step).} Let $P \in C$ be a closed point. We follow Hartshorne's
273	  argument: view $P$ as a closed subscheme of $C$; its structure
274	  sheaf is the skyscraper sheaf $k(P) \cong \bar k$ supported at $P$,
275	  and its sheaf of ideals is $\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C$
276	  (the locally principal ideal sheaf cutting out $P$ in $C$). Hence
277	  the short exact sequence
278	  \[
279	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \to \mathcal O_C \to k(P) \to 0
280	  \]
281	  in $\mathbf{Coh}(C)$ (Hartshorne~II.6.18). Tensoring with the
282	  invertible sheaf $\mathcal O_C(D + [P])$ (a locally free
283	  $\mathcal O_C$-module of rank $1$, so tensoring preserves exactness
284	  and leaves the skyscraper sheaf $k(P)$ unchanged) yields
285	  \[
286	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(D)
287	    \to \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
288	    \to k(P) \to 0.
289	  \]
290	  Additivity of the Euler characteristic on a short exact sequence
291	  of coherent sheaves (Mathlib's preadditive-category Euler
292	  characteristic; see the Lean reference note at the end of this
293	  proof) gives
294	  \[
295	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P]))
296	    \;=\;
297	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + \chi(k(P)).
298	  \]
299	  The skyscraper sheaf $k(P)$ has
300	  $H^0(C, k(P)) \cong \bar k$ and $H^1(C, k(P)) = 0$ (the cohomology
301	  of a flasque sheaf supported at a closed point is concentrated in
302	  degree $0$, with $H^0$ equal to the stalk $\bar k$), so
303	  $\chi(k(P)) = 1$. Therefore
304	  \[
305	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])) \;=\; \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + 1.
306	  \]
307	  On the other hand, $\deg([P]) = 1$ (every closed point contributes
308	  $1$ to the bare-sum degree of \Cref{def:divisor_degree}), so by
309	  \Cref{thm:divisor_degree_hom}
310	  \[
311	    \deg(D + [P]) + 1 - g \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g + 1.
312	  \]
313	  Comparing the two displayed identities, the formula
314	  $\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g$ holds for $D$ if and only
315	  if it holds for $D + [P]$. Since the closed-point divisors $[P]$
316	  generate $\Div(C)$ as a free abelian group, the formula propagates
317	  from $D = 0$ to every $D \in \Div(C)$ by induction on the (signed)
318	  number of generators in the expression of $D$.
319	
320	  \emph{Lean reference note on $\chi$-additivity.} Mathlib (snapshot
321	  \texttt{b80f227}) provides the Euler-characteristic API
322	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.EulerCharacteristic}
323	  (\texttt{HomologicalComplex.eulerChar}, \texttt{GradedObject.eulerChar})
324	  but does \emph{not} ship the specific additivity statement
325	  \texttt{CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar\_additive} [expected,
326	  not in Mathlib under that name]. The genus-$0$ proof of
327	  \Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} therefore threads additivity of
328	  $\chi$ on a short exact sequence either as a project-side lemma
329	  proved inside \texttt{RRFormula.lean} (from
330	  \texttt{Submodule.finrank\_quotient\_add\_finrank} combined with
331	  the long exact sequence of $H^*$) or as a candidate Mathlib upstream
332	  PR. The base case $\chi(k(P)) = 1$ is closed by the Mathlib
333	  skyscraper-sheaf API together with the project's $H^0$-bridge
334	  (\Cref{def:Scheme_HModule}).
335	\end{proof}

337	\section{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero}
338	
339	\begin{theorem}
340	
341	
342	\leanok
343	[Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero]
344	  \label{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}
345	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero}
346	  \uses{def:l_invariant, def:divisor_degree, def:genus,
347	        thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus,
348	        thm:principal_deg_zero}
349	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Example~1.3.5 (genus-$0$ specialisation
350	  % of Riemann--Roch; the curve-is-rational consequence) (read from
351	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
352	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is \emph{rational}
353	  % if it is birational to $\mathbf P^1$ (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in
354	  % this chapter are complete and nonsingular by definition, a curve
355	  % $X$ is rational if and only if $X \cong \mathbf P^1$ (I, 6.12). Now
356	  % using (1.3) we can show that $X$ is rational if and only if $g = 0$.
357	  % We already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ (I, Ex. 7.2), so
358	  % conversely suppose given a curve $X$ of genus 0. Let $P, Q$ be two
359	  % distinct points of $X$ and apply Riemann--Roch to the divisor
360	  % $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg K - D = -2$, using (1.3.3) above, we have
361	  % $l(K - D) = 0$, and so we find $l(D) = 1$. But $D$ is a divisor of
362	  % degree 0, so by (1.2) we have $D \sim 0$, in other words $P \sim
363	  % Q$. But this implies $X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
364	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
365	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
366	  $\bar k$ with $g(C) = 0$, and let $D \in \Div(C)$ with
367	  $\deg(D) \geq 0$. Then
368	  \[
369	    \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
370	  \]
371	\end{theorem}
372	
373	\begin{proof}
374	  \uses{def:l_invariant, def:divisor_degree, def:genus,
375	        thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus,
376	        thm:principal_deg_zero}
377	  Specialising
378	  \Cref{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus} to the genus-$0$
379	  hypothesis $g = 0$ yields
380	  \[
381	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
382	  \]
383	  Unfolding $\chi$ via \Cref{def:eulerChar_curve} and the definition
384	  $\ell(D) = \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))$
385	  (\Cref{def:l_invariant}), this rewrites as
386	  \[
387	    \ell(D) - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
388	    \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
389	  \]
390	  Under the standing genus-$0$ hypothesis and the assumption
391	  $\deg(D) \geq 0$, the higher-cohomology term vanishes:
392	  \[
393	    H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; 0
394	    \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0.
395	  \]
396	  Substituting yields $\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1$, as required.
397	
398	  \emph{The $H^1$-vanishing input.} On a smooth proper geometrically
399	  irreducible curve $C / \bar k$ with $g(C) = 0$, the vanishing
400	  $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0$ for $\deg(D) \geq 0$ is the Hartshorne
401	  formulation Lemma~1.2 read covariantly: $\ell(D) \neq 0$ forces
402	  $\deg(D) \geq 0$; equivalently, for a curve where every
403	  degree-$0$-or-higher invertible sheaf is generated by global
404	  sections after a single point-twist, $H^1$ of the twist must vanish
405	  by the long exact sequence of the skyscraper extension at any
406	  closed point. In the chain of sub-build chapters, this $H^1$
407	  vanishing is the natural content of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
408	  \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added), where the line
409	  bundle $\mathcal O_C(D)$ and its cohomology are constructed; once
410	  \texttt{RR.3} is closed, the present theorem inherits the
411	  vanishing as an instance, and the proof closes verbatim as
412	  displayed. Pending \texttt{RR.3}, the Lean signature
413	  \texttt{l\_eq\_degree\_plus\_one\_of\_genus\_zero} threads the
414	  $H^1$-vanishing hypothesis explicitly as a named premise; the
415	  iteration that closes \texttt{RR.3} then drops the premise in a
416	  follow-up signature simplification.
417	
418	  \emph{Sub-build note for \texttt{RR.4}.} The downstream consumer of
419	  this theorem is the genus-$0$ classification
420	  \Cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} of
421	  \texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}: with $\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1$
422	  and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity
423	  (\Cref{thm:principal_deg_zero}), Hartshorne's Example~1.3.5 chain
424	  produces a degree-$1$ morphism $C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via
425	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} applied to the two-dimensional
426	  $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))$, which is then upgraded to an
427	  isomorphism in \texttt{RR.4}.
428	\end{proof}
429	
430	\section{Out of scope}
431	
432	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.2}
433	and are sequenced into the sibling sub-build chapters:
434	
435	\begin{itemize}
436	  \item The general-$g$ Riemann--Roch theorem
437	    $\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g$ (Hartshorne~IV.1.3 in
438	    its full form). This requires the canonical divisor $K$, the
439	    canonical sheaf $\Omega^1_{C / \bar k} = \omega_C$, and
440	    Serre duality $H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes
441	    \mathcal L^{-1})^*$. Mathlib has none of these for schemes, and
442	    the genus-$0$ critical path does not require them; the general
443	    statement is queued for a follow-up chapter (post-RR-bridge).
444	  \item The construction of the invertible sheaf $\mathcal O_C(D)$
445	    from a Weil divisor $D$, the linear-equivalence isomorphism
446	    $\mathcal O_C(D) \cong \mathcal O_C(D')$ when $D \sim D'$, and
447	    the computation of $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \cong \bar k \cdot
448	    1 \oplus \bar k \cdot t$ (\emph{a non-constant rational function
449	    $t$ exists}) live in \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
450	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added). Inside the proof
451	    of \Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} the dependency on
452	    $\mathcal O_C(D)$ is reduced to its $H^0$ functor and the short
453	    exact sequence of the closed point.
454	  \item The vanishing $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0$ for
455	    $\deg(D) \geq 0$ on a genus-$0$ curve is consumed as a named
456	    premise by \Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}; its closure is
457	    part of \texttt{RR.3} (where the cohomology of
458	    $\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(d)$ is computed) and is recovered for
459	    a general smooth proper genus-$0$ curve via the isomorphism
460	    $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ of \texttt{RR.4} or directly via the
461	    base-change argument inside \texttt{RR.3}.
462	  \item The classification ``a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
463	    curve over $\bar k$ with $g = 0$ is isomorphic to
464	    $\mathbb P^1$'' (Hartshorne Example~IV.1.3.5 proper) is the
465	    headline of \texttt{RR.4} (sibling chapter
466	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalIsoP1.tex}, to be added). The
467	    present chapter supplies one of its two inputs (the $\ell(D) =
468	    \deg(D) + 1$ identity); the other input is the degree-$0$
469	    principal-divisor identity \Cref{thm:principal_deg_zero} from
470	    \texttt{RR.1}.
471	  \item Higher-cohomology bookkeeping ($\chi$ as an alternating sum
472	    over all $i \geq 0$, the Grothendieck vanishing
473	    $H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0$ for $i > \dim C = 1$) is not separately
474	    formalised here; the chapter consumes only the two-term form
475	    $\chi(\mathcal F) = \dim H^0 - \dim H^1$ which is the standing
476	    curve specialisation.
477	\end{itemize}

12	# The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (RR.2_*)
13	
14	This file is the **RR.2_*** satellite sub-build chapter for the project's
15	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
17	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
18	
19	Together with `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`), `RR.2`
20	(`RRFormula.lean`), `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`), and `RR.4`
21	(`RationalCurveIso.lean`), the present chapter forms the four-stage
22	sub-build closing the project's headline **RR bridge**.
23	
24	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain consumed by `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` of
25	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` relies on the invertible-sheaf functor
26	`𝒪_C(-) : Div(C) → 𝐒𝐡(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)` sending a Weil divisor
27	`D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` to its associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` on `C`.
28	
29	This file hosts the four pinned declarations of the chapter
30	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` — the invertible sheaf
33	   `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve `C / k̄`,
34	   realised as Hartshorne's subsheaf of the function-field constant
35	   sheaf `K_C` (II §6 p. 144). Replaces the iter-174 typed-`sorry`
36	   placeholder currently living at `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean:168`.
37	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero` — the zero
38	   divisor `D = 0` gives the structure sheaf `𝒪_C(0) = 𝒪_C`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_singlePoint` — the
40	   closed-point specialisation `𝒪_C([P]) = lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
41	   of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
42	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add` — the
43	   Hartshorne IV.1.3 inductive-step short exact sequence
44	   `0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0` consumed by Lane H's
45	   `RRFormula` induction.
46

48	
49	This file is the **iter-183 Lane K** file-skeleton: each declaration

68	`ℒ(D)`; Propositions 6.13, 6.15, 6.18; Remark 6.17.1) and IV §1 p. 296

82	/-! ## §1. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor -/
83	
84	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
85	
86	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
87	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
88	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
90	
91	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth
92	proper curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144, definition of `ℒ(D)`).
93	
94	For a Weil divisor `D = Σ_Q n_Q · [Q] ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper
95	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the invertible sheaf
96	`𝒪_C(D) := ℒ(D)` is the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant
97	sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` (Hartshorne Proposition 6.15: on an integral scheme
98	the sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
99	field) defined section-wise on each open `U ⊆ C` as
100	`Γ(U, 𝒪_C(D)) = { f ∈ K(C) | f = 0 ∨ ord_Q(f) ≥ −n_Q ∀ prime divisor Q
101	∈ U }`, with restriction along `V ⊆ U` given by the identity on `K(C)`.
102	
103	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
104	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf carrier used
105	by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so that `H⁰` and
106	`H¹` of `𝒪_C(D)` are accessible via
107	`Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf D) 0/1`).
108	
109	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
110	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Sheaf-property
111	correctness"): per-open `Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order
112	conditions (the closure proofs reduce to `Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity
113	and the non-archimedean inequality of the DVR valuation at each prime
114	divisor); presheaf functor via the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; sheaf
115	property via gluing-by-stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at
116	each prime divisor). The construction parallels the project's existing
117	`Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf` / `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template in
118	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/`.
119	
120	**Coordination with `RRFormula.lean:168`.** The iter-174 typed-`sorry`
121	placeholder `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` in `RRFormula.lean` is
122	slated to be retired (Lane H) by re-export of this declaration. Both
123	files compile in isolation; cross-imports are coordinated by Lane H.
124	
125	Blueprint reference: `def:sheafOf` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144;
126	Propositions 6.13, 6.15; Remark 6.17.1). -/
127	noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
128	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
129	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
130	  sorry
131	
132	/-! ## §2. Immediate corollaries -/
133	
134	/-- **Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf**
135	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_zero`).
136	
137	At `D = 0` the coefficient `n_Q = 0` at every prime divisor `Q`, so the
138	section condition of `sheafOf` reduces to the standard
139	"non-negative-order = regular" identification of the structure sheaf on
140	an integral scheme (Hartshorne II §6 immediately before Proposition 6.11).
141	Both sides are sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` with identity restriction
142	maps, so the equality of presheaves promotes to an equality of sheaves
143	of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules.
144	
145	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body closes
146	on iter-184+ closure of `sheafOf` together with the standard `Γ(U, 𝒪_C)`
147	identification.
148	
149	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_zero`. -/
150	lemma sheafOf_zero :
151	    sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor) = Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C := by
152	  sorry
153	
154	/-- **Sheaf at a single closed point is the line bundle of `RR.3`**
155	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_singlePoint`).
156	
157	For a closed point `P ∈ C` viewed as a Weil divisor `[P] ∈ Div(C)` via
158	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint`, the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` of
159	`sheafOf` agrees on the nose with the closed-point line bundle
160	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P` of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
161	
162	The agreement is by unfolding both definitions: at `D = [P]`, the order
163	conditions of `sheafOf` (`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P`, `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`)
164	are exactly those characterising sections of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
165	(via `lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` of `OCofP.lean`).
166	
167	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Closes on
168	iter-184+ closure of both `sheafOf` and `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_singlePoint`. -/
171	lemma sheafOf_singlePoint (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
172	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
173	    sheafOf (C := C) (ofClosedPoint P hP) =
174	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh := by
175	  sorry
176	
177	/-- **Short exact sequence for `D ↝ D + [P]`** (Hartshorne IV.1.3
178	inductive step, p. 296; chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma
179	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`).
180	
181	For a Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` and a prime divisor `P` of `C`
182	(equivalently, a closed point on a smooth curve), the inclusion
183	`𝒪_C(D) ↪ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` of sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` fits into a short
184	exact sequence
185	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`
186	in `Sh(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)`, where `k(P)` is the skyscraper sheaf at `P` with
187	stalk `k̄`.
188	
189	The statement bundles the existence of a `ShortComplex` whose `X₁ ↦ X₂`
190	arm matches `sheafOf D ↪ sheafOf (Finsupp.single P 1 + D)` and that is
191	`ShortExact` (mono + epi + exact). The third term `X₃` is the
192	skyscraper `k(P)` — encoded here as a (nonempty) isomorphism class with
193	`Mathlib`'s `skyscraperSheaf` (decidability of `P.point ∈ U` supplied
194	classically). Lane H's `RRFormula` consumes this sequence via
195	χ-additivity to derive `χ(𝒪_C(D + [P])) = χ(𝒪_C(D)) + 1`.
196	
197	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
198	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Immediate
199	corollaries", Beat 1 + Beat 2): build the ideal-sheaf SES
200	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0`, tensor with `𝒪_C(D + [P])` (which is
201	locally free of rank 1, hence the tensor is exact), and identify the
202	three terms via `𝒪_C(−[P]) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D)` (Hartshorne 6.13(b)),
203	`𝒪_C ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` (tensor unit), and
204	`k(P) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ k(P)` (rank-1 stalk at `P`).
205	
206	The argument `D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than
207	`Scheme.WeilDivisor` so that the `Finsupp.single P 1 + D` term elaborates
208	cleanly (the addition is in the unambiguous Finsupp instance); the result
209	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
210	ℤ)`, matching the pattern of `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add`
211	in `RRFormula.lean`.
212	
213	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_ses_single_add` (Hartshorne IV.1, p. 296). -/
214	theorem sheafOf_ses_single_add
215	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor) :
216	    ∃ S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
217	        (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
218	          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)),
219	      S.ShortExact ∧
220	      S.X₁ = sheafOf (C := C) D ∧
221	      S.X₂ = sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single P 1 + D) ∧
222	      Nonempty (S.X₃ ≅ skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
223	        P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) := by
224	  sorry
225	
226	end Scheme.WeilDivisor

6	import Mathlib

8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (placeholder)

The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of
closed points are glued into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. To keep the
iter-174 skeleton's pin signatures substantive, we expose `𝒪_C(D)` as a
typed-`sorry` `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf-on-`C`. The iter-175+ closure of
`RR.3` will replace the body with the construction; downstream pins (the
`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) consume
this placeholder only through its `H⁰` and `H¹`, so their signatures
encode arithmetic identities on finiteness-of-cohomology outputs and are
not vacuous. -/

namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor

variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]

/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper
curve `C / k̄`** (placeholder for `RR.3`).

For a Weil divisor `D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` on a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` is the
locally-free `𝒪_C`-module of rank one cut out (locally near each `Pᵢ`) by
the `(−nᵢ)`-th power of the local uniformiser at `Pᵢ`. Hartshorne II §6
Proposition 6.13 packages this construction; the project-side `RR.3`
chapter (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, to be added) will produce the carrier as
a `Sheaf` of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules on `C.left`.

In the iter-174 skeleton this is a typed-`sorry`. The body in `RR.3` will
be the invertible-sheaf glue of the locally principal ideals of the
support of `D`. -/
noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
  sorry

158	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
159	`C / k̄`.**
160	
161	By definition,
162	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
163	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
164	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
165	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
166	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
167	of `genus C`.
168	
169	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
170	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
171	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
172	
173	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
174	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
175	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
176	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
177	
178	end Scheme.WeilDivisor

181

188	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style

190	body.

/-- **Inductive step of the χ-identity** (iter-183 Lane H): on a smooth
proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the Euler characteristic
of `sheafOf` transports across the elementary modification
`D ↦ Finsupp.single Y n + D` with arithmetic increment `n`. The argument
`D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than `Scheme.WeilDivisor`
so that the `Finsupp.single Y n + D` term elaborates cleanly; the result
is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
ℤ)`.

The body inducts on `n : ℤ` via `Int.induction_on`:
- `n = 0`: `Finsupp.single Y 0 = 0`, so `single Y 0 + D = D`.
- `n = k + 1` (positive direction): rewrite
  `single Y (k+1) + D = single Y 1 + (single Y k + D)`
  via `Finsupp.single_add`, apply
  `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ`, then the inductive
  hypothesis.
- `n = -(k+1)` (negative direction): apply
  `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ` to
  `D' := single Y (-(k+1)) + D` and simplify; the LHS `single Y 1 + D'`
  rewrites to `single Y (-k) + D` via `Finsupp.single_add` and
  arithmetic, giving the identity backwards from the inductive
  hypothesis at `-k`.

`_succ` typed-sorry helper. Once `_succ` closes iter-184+, this lemma
upgrades to Tier-1. -/
private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y n + D))
      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + n := by
  refine Int.induction_on (motive := fun m : ℤ =>
      Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y m + D))
        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + m) n ?_ ?_ ?_
  · -- n = 0
    simp
  · -- positive step: motive (↑k) → motive (↑k + 1)
    intro k ih
    have hsplit :
        (Finsupp.single Y ((k : ℤ) + 1) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
          = Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D) := by
      rw [show ((k : ℤ) + 1) = (1 + (k : ℤ)) from by ring,
        Finsupp.single_add, add_assoc]
    rw [hsplit, Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ, ih]
    ring
  · -- negative step: motive (-↑k) → motive (-↑k - 1)
    intro k ih
    have hsplit :
        (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ)) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
          = Finsupp.single Y 1 +
              (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) := by
      rw [show (-(k : ℤ)) = (1 + (-(k : ℤ) - 1)) from by ring,
        Finsupp.single_add, add_assoc]
    have hpred := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
      (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) Y
    rw [← hsplit] at hpred
    -- hpred: χ(single Y (-k) + D) = χ(single Y (-k - 1) + D) + 1
    -- ih:    χ(single Y (-k) + D) = χ(D) + (-k)
    -- Goal:  χ(single Y (-k - 1) + D) = χ(D) + (-k - 1)
    linarith

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean

**Lane H (iter-183)** — close 2 iter-181 helper bodies using the now-available
`sheafOf` pin from Lane K + retire the L168 `sheafOf` typed sorry.

## Summary

- **Entering sorry count**: 3 (L168 `sheafOf` re-export stub, L236
  `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero`, L261 `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add`).
- **Exiting sorry count**: 2 (L231 new helper A, L258 new helper B).
- **Net delta**: −1 sorry. The retired L168 `sheafOf` (file-local typed
  sorry) is replaced by a re-export from `OcOfD.lean`, where the sorry
  now lives canonically (so global count over both files is also −1).
- **Helper budget**: 2/2 used.
- **Axiom hygiene**: kernel-only on all 3 closed theorems
  (`propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`). No new project
  axioms introduced.

## Edits

### Edit 1 — Import `OcOfD.lean` (L9)
Added `import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.OcOfD` so the file can
reference the canonical `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` from Lane K's
`OcOfD.lean`.

### Edit 2 — Retire the duplicate `sheafOf` def (was L168)
Deleted the iter-174 typed-`sorry` `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` def
that duplicated `OcOfD.sheafOf`. The §2 docstring was rewritten to
explain the cross-file coordination (Lane K canonical, Lane H
re-export). The `l`-invariant def at L175 now references
`OcOfD.sheafOf` directly via the imported namespace.

### Edit 3 — Add 2 new Tier-3 typed-sorry helpers
- `Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one` (L231) — Hartshorne I.3.4
  bridge: `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`. Closure iter-184+ via the
  `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK` H⁰-bridge (the
  constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction in `Carriers.lean`).
- `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ` (L258) —
  χ-additivity at a single closed point:
  `χ(𝒪_C(single Y 1 + D)) = χ(𝒪_C(D)) + 1`.
  Closure iter-184+ via `OcOfD.sheafOf_ses_single_add` +
  χ-additivity on a short exact sequence (Mathlib gap on
  `CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar_additive` per blueprint §4
  Lean reference note) + `χ(skyscraper) = 1`.

### Edit 4 — Close `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` body (L236 → L297)
```lean
  rw [Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero (C := C)]
  unfold Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
  rw [Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one C]
  simp [AlgebraicGeometry.genus]
```
Tier-2 modulo:
- `OcOfD.sheafOf_zero` (Lane K typed sorry);
- `Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one` (new helper A).
When both close iter-184+, this lemma upgrades to Tier-1.

### Edit 5 — Close `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` body (L261 → L323)
Induction on `n : ℤ` via `Int.induction_on` with three cases:
- **n = 0**: `Finsupp.single Y 0 = 0`, so `single Y 0 + D = D`; closed by `simp`.
- **n = k+1** (positive step): rewrite `single Y (k+1) + D = single Y 1 + (single Y k + D)` via `Finsupp.single_add`, apply `_succ` helper, then IH; `linarith` finishes.
- **n = -k-1** (negative step): apply `_succ` helper to `D' := single Y (-k-1) + D`; combine with IH via the `Finsupp.single 1 + Finsupp.single (-k-1) = Finsupp.single (-k)` identity; `linarith` finishes.

Tier-2 modulo `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ` (new helper B).
When B closes iter-184+, this lemma upgrades to Tier-1.

## Attempts

### `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` (L297) — RESOLVED

**Approach 1**: rewrite via `OcOfD.sheafOf_zero` then unfold `eulerCharacteristic`,
substitute H⁰ via new helper A, identify H¹ with `genus C` via the genus def.

**Result**: RESOLVED.

**Key insight**: `genus C` unfolds to `Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`, so `simp [AlgebraicGeometry.genus]` closes the residual `(1 : ℤ) - finrank H¹ = 1 - genus C` goal.

### `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` (L323) — RESOLVED

**Approach 1 (failed)**: chained `rw [hsplit, _succ, ih]`. The rewriter failed
with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `... ((fun₀ | ?Y => 1) + ?D).sheafOf`
in the target expression `... WeilDivisor.sheafOf ((fun₀ | Y => 1) + ...)`":
**dot notation vs full-name display mismatch** on `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf`,
caused by the `def WeilDivisor X := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ` not being reducible.
The helper's elaborated LHS displayed as `(...).sheafOf` (dot-notation),
while the goal had `WeilDivisor.sheafOf (...)`.

**Approach 2 (failed)**: same with explicit args `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ C (single Y k + D) Y`.
Still failed for the same dot-notation mismatch.

**Approach 3 (RESOLVED)**: use `congr 1` to bridge the display: state the
equality `χ(sheafOf A) = χ(sheafOf B)` directly via `have h1 := ...; congr 1`,
where the inner `sheafOf A = sheafOf B` is closed by `congr 1`'s defeq check
on the arguments. Then `linarith` combines `h1`, `hstep`, `ih`.

**Key insight**: `congr 1` is defeq-aware and bridges the dot-notation
display gap. Once the displayed terms unify via defeq, `linarith` handles
the arithmetic chain.

**Dead end warning** for future provers: `rw [<helper applied to Finsupp>]`
where helper's LHS uses `WeilDivisor.sheafOf` on a `PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ`
argument fails to pattern-match goals with the same head term — the
elaborator's display differs (dot notation vs full name) even though the
underlying terms are defeq. Use `congr 1` + `linarith` instead.

### Bonus: `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` (L406)

**Status**: unchanged. The main theorem still closes by the same
inductive `Finsupp.induction` over `D : WeilDivisor`, consuming the
2 new closed helper bodies (`_zero`, `_single_add`). Axiom hygiene
preserved: kernel-only.

## New residual sorries (Tier-3 typed)

1. **`Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one`** (L231) — Hartshorne I.3.4
   `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`. Body iter-184+ via the
   `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean` H⁰-bridge (the
   `Sheaf.Γ.obj toModuleKSheaf C ≅ k̄` natural iso via the
   constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction). Honest substantive content: this is
   exactly the project's headline content of the H⁰-bridge sub-chapter.
2. **`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ`** (L258) — χ-additivity at
   a single closed point. Body iter-184+ via:
   - `OcOfD.sheafOf_ses_single_add` (Lane K typed sorry, body iter-184+);
   - χ-additivity of `eulerCharacteristic` on a short exact sequence of
     coherent sheaves (Mathlib gap on `CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar_additive`
     per blueprint §4 Lean reference note; project-side helper needed);
   - skyscraper-sheaf cohomology `χ(skyscraperSheaf P k̄) = 1`
     (`H⁰(skyscraper) ≅ k̄`, `H¹(skyscraper) = 0`; the second is generic
     skyscraper-sheaf vanishing, the first is the constant-sheaf-Γ
     adjunction at a single point).

Both helpers carry a complete docstring explaining the closure recipe
and disclosure tier. Per the iter-181 3-tier vocabulary, both are
