content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.

102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.

165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
281	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
282	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
283	
284	The body is iter-183+ work; see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` for the
285	construction route via affine-chart-localised `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
286	(Stacks tag `02RW` / `0AX5`). The substantive content — the assignment from
287	a morphism `φ` to its `[∞]`-pullback Weil divisor — is the named target of
288	`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`: the latter pins the existential to this
289	specific divisor (not just any positive-degree divisor on `C`). -/
290	noncomputable def Hom.poleDivisor
291	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
292	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
293	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
294	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
295	    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
296	    C.left.WeilDivisor := sorry
297	
298	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
299	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
300	under finite pullback).
301	
302	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
303	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
304	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
305	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
306	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
307	
308	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
309	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
310	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
311	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
312	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
313	
314	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
315	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
316	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
317	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
318	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
319	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
320	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
321	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
322	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
323	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
324	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
325	
326	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
327	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).
328	
329	**Signature strengthening (iter-182 Pin 2).** The iter-177 file-skeleton
330	signature output existential `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = (d : ℤ)` was
331	flagged `weakened-wrong` by the iter-181 `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`:
332	the existential is discharged by ANY positive-degree divisor on `C` and
333	does not reference `φ`. iter-182 strengthens the output to pin `D` to
334	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and
335	pin its degree to `Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
336	C.left.functionField` (the function-field-extension degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]`).
337	The `[Algebra ...]` instance binder pins the intended `Algebra` structure
338	to be the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map (matching the iter-181
339	Pin 3 sig refinement convention). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`
340	(verdict PROCEED) for the body recipe via
341	`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`. -/
342	theorem morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor
343	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
344	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
345	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
346	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
347	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
348	    (_hφ_non_const :
349	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
350	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
351	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField] :
352	    ∃ (D : C.left.WeilDivisor),
353	      D = Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ ∧
354	        Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D =
355	          (Module.finrank
356	            (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
357	            C.left.functionField : ℤ) := by
358	  sorry

360	/-! ## §3. Pin 3 — degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
361	is an isomorphism (`iso_of_degree_one`)
362	
363	Hartshorne I §6 Corollary 6.12 (the function-field ⇔ smooth projective
364	curve equivalence of categories) specialised to a non-constant
365	degree-`1` morphism: such a morphism induces an isomorphism on function
366	fields, and lifts to an isomorphism of schemes by the equivalence.
367	
368	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
369	is now encoded directly as
370	`Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1` paired with an
371	`[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass binder (the
372	intended instance at each call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
373	function-field map). This refines the iter-177 file-skeleton
374	placeholder hypothesis
375	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)`, which is strictly
376	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs (the birational-extension
377	argument requires the iso to be *induced by `φ` itself*, not an
378	abstract ring iso). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
379	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis. The body in iter-182+ uses
380	either Hartshorne I.6.12 (the equivalence of categories) or the
381	Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` route to
382	lift the function-field iso to a scheme iso. -/
383	
384	/-- **Degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is an
385	isomorphism** (Hartshorne I Corollary 6.12 specialised to degree `1`,
386	or equivalently Stacks tag `0AVX`).
387	
388	For smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves `C, C'` over an
389	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
390	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
391	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
392	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
393	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.
394	
395	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
396	is encoded as a typeclass binder
397	`[Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]` together with
398	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
399	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
400	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
401	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
402	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
403	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
404	an abstract existence wrapper
405	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
406	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
407	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
408	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
409	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
410	
411	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
412	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
413	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
414	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
415	
416	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
417	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
418	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
419	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
420	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
421	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
422	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
423	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
424	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
425	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
426	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).
427	4. Compose to extract `C ≅ C'` in the slice category.
428	
429	Alternative route (cleaner conceptually but more sheaf API): the
430	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic
431	rank `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`; the inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an
432	iso of coherent rank-`1` sheaves (torsion-free coherent of generic
433	rank `0` is zero on a Dedekind base), so `φ_* O_C = O_{C'}` and `φ`
434	is the structure morphism of an iso.
435	
436	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_one_morphism_iso` (Hartshorne I §6
437	Corollary 6.12 p. 45 + IV §2 opening p. 299; Stacks tag `0AVX`). -/
438	theorem iso_of_degree_one
439	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
440	    {C C' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
441	    [IsProper C.hom] [IsProper C'.hom]
442	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C'.hom]
443	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C'.hom]
444	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral C'.left]
445	    (φ : C ⟶ C')
446	    (_hφ_non_const : ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C',
447	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
448	    [Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]
449	    (_hφ_deg :
450	      Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1) :
451	    Nonempty (C ≅ C') :=
452	  sorry
453	
454	end Scheme
455	
456	end AlgebraicGeometry

3	\chapter{The rational-curve isomorphism $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ (RR.4)}
4	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}
5	
6	% SOURCE: Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, IV \S1 Example~1.3.5
7	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314)
8	% + IV \S2, opening of "Hurwitz's Theorem" (degree of a finite morphism of
9	% curves is the function-field extension degree; read from PDF page 316)
10	% + I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field $\Leftrightarrow$ smooth-projective-curve
11	% equivalence of categories; read from PDF page 62). The verbatim quotes
12	% pasted in the declaration blocks below were copied character-by-character
13	% from the rendered page images of the scanned PDF (no text layer is present).
14	
15	\section{Setup and motivation}
16	
17	This chapter is the fourth and final sub-build chapter
18	\texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4} closing the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
19	\[
20	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
21	  \,\colon\,
22	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C / \bar k$ with
23	  $g(C) = 0$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.}
24	\]
25	The chain assembled across the four sub-build chapters is:
26	\begin{itemize}
27	  \item \texttt{RR.1} (\Cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}): the Weil-divisor
28	    group $\Div(C)$, the degree map
29	    $\deg \colon \Div(C) \to \mathbb Z$ (\Cref{def:divisor_degree}), and the
30	    degree-zero principal-divisor identity
31	    (\Cref{thm:principal_deg_zero}).
32	  \item \texttt{RR.2} (\Cref{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}): the Riemann--Roch
33	    formula in genus zero $\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1$ for $\deg(D) \geq 0$
34	    (\Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}).
35	  \item \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter, dispatched parallel iter-174):
36	    the line bundle $\mathcal O_C([P])$, its global-sections identification
37	    $\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) = 2$, and the corollary
38	    \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} producing a non-constant
39	    rational function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
40	    \bar k \cdot 1$ on a genus-$0$ curve with a marked $\bar k$-point.
41	  \item \texttt{RR.4} (\emph{this chapter}): the global classification ``a
42	    smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C / \bar k$ with $g(C) = 0$
43	    and a $\bar k$-rational point is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.''
44	\end{itemize}
45	This chapter consumes \texttt{RR.3} (the non-constant function) and produces
46	the Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} pinned at
47	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean} line~290 and restated as
48	the interface block \Cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} of
49	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}. The \texttt{RR.4}-closure of that target
50	also drops the \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
51	\texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme} via
52	\Cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}.
53	
54	\paragraph{Scope of the $\bar k$-base classification.} The chapter restricts
55	to the case where the base field $\bar k$ is algebraically closed. Over $\bar
56	k$ every closed point is $\bar k$-rational, so the ``$C$ has a $\bar
57	k$-rational point'' hypothesis of Hartshorne's Exercise~IV.1.3 is automatic
58	(the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C / \bar k$ is non-empty,
59	hence has closed points, all of which are $\bar k$-rational). The general
60	``$k \to \bar k$ descent'' step ``genus-$0$ curve over a non-closed $k$ with
61	a $k$-rational point $\cong \mathbb P^1_k$'' is a separate sub-build hosted
62	in \texttt{Jacobian.tex} (the \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key} downstream
63	consumer) and is out of scope here.
64	
65	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, $\bar k$ is an
66	algebraically closed field and $C$ is a smooth proper geometrically
67	irreducible curve over $\bar k$. The Lean signature
68	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) threads the
69	project's standing curve typeclass package:
70	\begin{quote}
71	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
72	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
73	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
74	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]}
75	\end{quote}
76	matching the typeclass set used by the headline target
77	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} in
78	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290--296}. The genus
79	$g(C)$ is the one defined in \Cref{def:genus} of \texttt{Genus.tex},
80	namely $g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ via the project's
81	$\Module \bar k$-flavoured sheaf cohomology
82	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}); the concrete projective line target
83	$\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ is the project's
84	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar~kbar} of
85	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean}.

87	\section{The morphism to $\mathbb P^1$ from two global sections}
88	
89	\begin{lemma}
90	
91	\leanok
92	[Morphism to $\mathbb P^1$ from a globally generating pair of sections]
93	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
94	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
95	  \uses{def:codim1_cycles}
96	  Let $X$ be a scheme over a field $\bar k$, let $\mathcal L$ be an invertible
97	  sheaf on $X$, and let $s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)$ be two global
98	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
99	  \[
100	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
101	  \]
102	  (Equivalently, $s_0$ and $s_1$ generate $\mathcal L$ at every point.) Then
103	  there exists a unique morphism of $\bar k$-schemes
104	  \[
105	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
106	  \]
107	  whose pullback of $\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)$ is $\mathcal L$ and whose
108	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
109	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))$ are $s_0$ and $s_1$ respectively. On the
110	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
111	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ the morphism $\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}$ is the map ``$x
112	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)$''; on the chart $D_+(x_1)$ it is the map ``$x
113	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
114	  $D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}$ by the cocycle identity
115	  $(s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1$.
116	\end{lemma}
117	
118	\begin{proof}
119	  \uses{def:codim1_cycles}
120	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
121	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to $\mathbb P^n$''), specialised to
122	  $n = 1$. Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
123	  under the name
124	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
125	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
126	
127	  \begin{quote}
128	  \small
129	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{$\sigma$ A\} [CommRing A]
130	    [SetLike $\sigma$ A] [AddSubgroupClass $\sigma$ A] ($\mathcal A$ :
131	    \(\mathbb N\) → $\sigma$) [GradedRing $\mathcal A$] \{X : Scheme\}\\
132	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj $\top$)\\
133	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant $\mathcal A$).toIdeal = $\top$) :\\
134	    \phantom{}\quad(X $\longrightarrow$ Proj $\mathcal A$)}
135	  \end{quote}
136	
137	  Specialising to $\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]$ with the standard $\mathbb
138	  N$-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded $\bar k$-algebra
139	  homomorphism $f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)$ and a
140	  proof that the irrelevant ideal $(x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]$ maps to
141	  the unit ideal of $H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)$ (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
142	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
143	  P^1_{\bar k}$ (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of $\mathbb
144	  P^n$ as a $\mathrm{Proj}$). When $\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
145	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
146	  cover $X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)$, where on each chart the sections become
147	  honest global functions ($s_1 / s_0$ on $D(s_0)$, $s_0 / s_1$ on $D(s_1)$,
148	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
149	  project-bespoke wrapper
150	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
151	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
152	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
153	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
154	
155	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
156	  a separated scheme: two morphisms $\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1$
157	  with $\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i$ for $i = 0, 1$ agree on the basic-open
158	  charts $D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)$ (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
159	  the same rational function), and these charts cover $\mathbb P^1$, so the
160	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
161	\end{proof}
162	
163	\section{Degree of a morphism to $\mathbb P^1$ via its pole divisor}
164	
165	% NOTE (iter-182 plan-phase Pin 2 signature strengthening). The iter-177
166	% file-skeleton placeholder existential
167	%   "$\exists (d : \mathbb N) (D : C.\mathrm{WeilDivisor}),\
168	%      0 < d \wedge D.\mathrm{degree} = d$"
169	% was vacuous in $\varphi$ (no constraint tying $D$ to $\varphi$ or the
170	% point $\infty$). The iter-182 plan-phase refactor (refactor slug
171	% `pin2-sig-strengthen`) replaces it with the substantive existential
172	% pinning $D$ to $\varphi^*[\infty]$ and the degree to the function-field
173	% extension degree $[K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]$. The new typed-sorry def
174	% `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` (in
175	% `RationalCurveIso.lean`, analogous to the iter-181 typed-sorry def
176	% `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` of OCofP.lean) carries the
177	% Weil divisor $\varphi^*[\infty]$; its body is queued for iter-183+
178	% via the affine-chart `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` route.
179	\begin{lemma}
180	
181	\leanok
182	[Degree of a non-constant morphism to $\mathbb P^1$ via the preimage of $\infty$]
183	  \label{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor}
184	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor}
185	  \uses{def:divisor_degree, def:order_at_point, def:divisor_closed_point}
186	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S2, opening paragraph of "Hurwitz's Theorem"
187	  % (definition of the degree of a finite morphism of curves) (read from
188	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
189	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
190	  % $f: X \to Y$ of curves is defined to be the degree $[K(X):K(Y)]$ of the
191	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
192	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.2, p.~299, opening of \S2.}
193	  Let $\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ be a non-constant morphism
194	  from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C / \bar k$. Then
195	  $\varphi$ is finite, its function-field extension
196	  $\bar k(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)$ (the canonical
197	  $\varphi$-induced map, supplied as an
198	  \texttt{[Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField C.functionField]}
199	  instance argument on the Lean side) is finite, and there exists a Weil
200	  divisor $D \in \Div(C)$ such that
201	  \[
202	    D \;=\; \varphi^*[\infty]
203	    \quad\text{and}\quad
204	    \deg(D) \;=\; [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,].
205	  \]
206	  Equivalently, the morphism $\varphi$ satisfies
207	  \[
208	    [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,] \;=\;
209	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
210	                  \ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [P]\,\right),
211	  \]
212	  where $t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)$ is a local parameter at $\infty$
213	  (equivalently $t_\infty = 1/u$ for the standard affine coordinate $u$ on
214	  $\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$); and for any closed point
215	  $Q \in \mathbb P^1$ the same identification gives
216	  $\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]$.
217	
218	  \emph{Convention on the pole divisor.} The Weil divisor
219	  $\varphi^*[\infty]$ is exposed in Lean as
220	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor $\varphi$}, a
221	  noncomputable definition introduced (as a typed \texttt{sorry}) at the
222	  iter-182 plan-phase signature strengthening of this lemma. The body of
223	  \texttt{poleDivisor} is queued for iter-183+ closure via the standard
224	  affine-chart construction (\texttt{Ideal.sum\_ramification\_inertia}
225	  in Mathlib, applied at each closed point $P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$
226	  to read off the local ramification multiplicity
227	  $\ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty)$ from the maximal ideal at $\infty$ in
228	  $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$). On the Lean side, the existential output of
229	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} packages
230	  $D = \texttt{Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor}\;\varphi$ together with
231	  $\deg(D) = \operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(\mathbb P^1)} K(C)$,
232	  mirroring the OCofP pattern in which a typed-sorry def
233	  (\Cref{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}) is introduced
234	  upstream of its consumer lemma.
235	\end{lemma}
236	
237	\begin{proof}
238	  \uses{def:divisor_degree, def:order_at_point, def:divisor_closed_point,
239	        thm:principal_deg_zero}
240	  A non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves over $\bar k$ is
241	  automatically finite. Indeed, the image of $\varphi$ is a closed
242	  irreducible subset of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ that is not a point (else
243	  $\varphi$ would be constant) and not empty, so it is all of $\mathbb
244	  P^1_{\bar k}$ (the only proper irreducible closed subset of $\mathbb
245	  P^1_{\bar k}$ is a point); hence $\varphi$ is surjective. A surjective
246	  morphism between smooth proper irreducible curves of equal dimension is
247	  finite (proper + quasi-finite, where quasi-finiteness comes from the
248	  fact that $\varphi$ has zero-dimensional fibres: a positive-dimensional
249	  fibre would force a one-dimensional irreducible component in the fibre,
250	  contradicting irreducibility of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ and $C$ at
251	  generic points). Hence the function-field extension
252	  $\bar k(\mathbb P^1) = \varphi^\# K(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)$
253	  is finite.
254	
255	  The identification $\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]$ for
256	  any closed point $Q \in \mathbb P^1$ is the multiplicativity of the
257	  degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9): for a finite morphism
258	  $\varphi \colon C \to C'$ of smooth proper curves and any divisor $D \in
259	  \Div(C')$, $\deg(\varphi^* D) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg(D)$. Specialising
260	  to $D = [Q]$ (a divisor of degree $1$ since $\kappa(Q) = \bar k$) gives
261	  $\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = \deg(\varphi)$. Equivalently, the degree of
262	  $\varphi$ equals the number of preimages of any closed point of $\mathbb
263	  P^1$, counted with the ramification multiplicities $\ord_P(\varphi^*
264	  t_\infty)$ at the preimage points $P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$.
265	
266	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The lemma threads two auxiliary inputs:
267	  ``non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is finite'' (the
268	  surjective-and-proper-and-quasi-finite argument above; Hartshorne~II.6.8)
269	  and ``multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback'' (Hartshorne~II.6.9
270	  and the multiplicativity of $\deg$ under composition of $\div$ and
271	  $\deg$; the closely related ``$\div(f)$ has degree zero'' specialisation
272	  is \Cref{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}). Both are separable
273	  sub-lemmas inside \texttt{RR.4}'s Lean file
274	  (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) and are
275	  slotted for closure in a follow-up iteration after the
276	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} skeleton lands.
277	\end{proof}

279	\section{A degree-$1$ morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism}
280	
281	% NOTE (iter-181 Lane I signature refinement; see analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md
282	% Decision 2, verdict DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). The iter-177 file-skeleton
283	% placeholder hypothesis "Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)"
284	% (an abstract function-field iso, unrelated to $\varphi$) was strictly
285	% weaker than the birational-extension body needs and was refined in
286	% iter-181 to the explicit function-field-extension-degree formulation
287	% [K(C):K(C')] = 1, i.e. Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
288	% = 1, with C.functionField a C'.functionField-algebra via the canonical
289	% \varphi-induced map (Scheme.Hom.stalkMap at the generic point composed
290	% with IsFractionRing.lift). The proof-prose body below (the Hartshorne
291	% I.6.12 chain + scheme-theoretic alternative argument) is unchanged ---
292	% only the input shape changes.
293	\begin{lemma}
294	
295	\leanok
296	[Degree-$1$ morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism]
297	  \label{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso}
298	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one}
299	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field-to-curve
300	  % equivalence of categories) (read from
301	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 62, doc p.~45)
302	  % + [Hartshorne], IV \S2 opening paragraph (degree of a finite morphism
303	  % of curves is the function-field extension degree) (read from
304	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
305	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.12. \emph{The following three categories are
306	  % equivalent:}
307	  % (i) \emph{nonsingular projective curves, and dominant morphisms;}
308	  % (ii) \emph{quasi-projective curves, and dominant rational maps;}
309	  % (iii) \emph{function fields of dimension 1 over $k$, and $k$-homomorphisms.}"
310	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
311	  % $f: X \to Y$ of curves is defined to be the degree $[K(X):K(Y)]$ of the
312	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
313	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, I.6 Corollary~6.12 (p.~45) + IV.2 opening
314	  (p.~299).}
315	  Let $C, C'$ be smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves over an
316	  algebraically closed field $\bar k$, and let $\varphi \colon C \to C'$ be
317	  a non-constant morphism whose induced function-field extension is of
318	  degree one,
319	  \[
320	    [\,K(C) : K(C')\,] \;=\; 1,
321	  \]
322	  formally
323	  $\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(C')} K(C) = 1$ in the Lean signature.
324	  Then $\varphi$ is an isomorphism of $\bar k$-schemes.
325	
326	  \emph{Convention on the function-field map.} At each call-site of this
327	  lemma, the $K(C')$-algebra structure on $K(C)$ used to interpret the
328	  degree $[K(C) : K(C')]$ is the canonical $\varphi$-induced
329	  function-field map, i.e.\ the composite
330	  \[
331	    K(C') \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{germ}\;}\;
332	    \mathcal O_{C', \varphi(\xi_C)}
333	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\varphi^\#\;}\;
334	    \mathcal O_{C, \xi_C}
335	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{frac.}\,\text{lift}\;}\; K(C)
336	  \]
337	  of the germ at the image of the generic point $\xi_C \in C$ with the
338	  stalk-map of $\varphi$ at $\xi_C$ and the fraction-field lift at
339	  $\xi_C$ (per \texttt{analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md}, Decision~2). On
340	  the Lean side this canonical instance is carried as a
341	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
342	  to \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one}; consumers
343	  supply it explicitly at each call-site.
344	\end{lemma}
345	
346	\begin{proof}
347	  The function-field hypothesis $[K(C) : K(C')] = 1$ states that the
348	  canonical $\varphi$-induced map $\varphi^\# \colon K(C') \to K(C)$ is a
349	  finite extension of degree one; since $K(C')$ is a field and the
350	  extension has dimension one as a $K(C')$-vector space, $\varphi^\#$ is
351	  an isomorphism of function fields $K(C') \cong K(C)$. (Equivalently, by
352	  the IV.2-opening identification $\deg(\varphi) = [K(C):K(C')]$, this is
353	  the statement $\deg(\varphi) = 1$; the morphism $\varphi$ is finite in
354	  this case by the standard argument that a non-constant morphism of
355	  smooth proper curves is finite, identical to the
356	  \Cref{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} preamble.)
357	
358	  By Hartshorne's Corollary~I.6.12, the assignment $C \mapsto \bar k(C)$ is
359	  an equivalence of categories between smooth projective curves over $\bar
360	  k$ (with dominant morphisms) and function fields of transcendence degree
361	  one over $\bar k$ (with $\bar k$-homomorphisms). Under this equivalence,
362	  the isomorphism $\bar k(C') \cong \bar k(C)$ produced by the previous
363	  paragraph lifts to a unique isomorphism of smooth proper curves $C \to
364	  C'$, and this isomorphism agrees with $\varphi$ on the generic point (by
365	  construction: it is the inverse-image of $\varphi^\#$ under the
366	  equivalence). The two morphisms $C \to C'$ that agree on the generic
367	  point of the integral scheme $C$ agree everywhere (a morphism out of an
368	  integral scheme to a separated scheme is determined by its restriction
369	  to the generic point, Hartshorne~II Ex.~4.2). Hence $\varphi$ is itself
370	  the lift, and is therefore an isomorphism.
371	
372	  \emph{Alternative direct argument.} Without invoking the
373	  equivalence-of-categories framing, the same conclusion is obtained
374	  scheme-theoretically: a finite degree-$1$ morphism $\varphi \colon C \to
375	  C'$ between integral normal noetherian schemes is an isomorphism.
376	  Indeed, the direct image $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C$ is a coherent
377	  $\mathcal O_{C'}$-module that is generically of rank $\deg(\varphi) =
378	  1$, and an inclusion $\mathcal O_{C'} \hookrightarrow \varphi_*
379	  \mathcal O_C$ of coherent rank-$1$ sheaves whose generic ranks agree
380	  is an isomorphism over the locus where the quotient
381	  $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C / \mathcal O_{C'}$ has trivial stalk. The
382	  quotient is a torsion coherent $\mathcal O_{C'}$-module of generic rank
383	  $\deg(\varphi) - 1 = 0$, hence is itself torsion-free (a
384	  finitely-generated torsion module over a Dedekind domain of generic
385	  rank zero is zero), so vanishes. Therefore $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C =
386	  \mathcal O_{C'}$ and $\varphi$ is the structure morphism of an
387	  isomorphism. Cf.\ Stacks tag~\textbf{0AVX} for the categorical version.
388	
389	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The Lean signature
390	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one} consumes a
391	  non-constant morphism $\varphi \colon C \to C'$ together with a
392	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
393	  (the canonical $\varphi$-induced function-field map) and the
394	  function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis
395	  \texttt{Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1}, and
396	  produces an isomorphism $C \cong C'$ in the slice category
397	  $\mathtt{Over (Spec (.of\ kbar))}$. The proof body threads the Mathlib
398	  API \texttt{IsIntegralHom.iso\_of\_isUnit} (or the equivalent
399	  \texttt{IsAffineHom + Finite + RankOne} chain) and, where needed, the
400	  project-bespoke ``finite-morphism-of-degree-one is an isomorphism''
401	  helper. The Hartshorne~I.6.12 equivalence-of-categories framing is the
402	  intended high-level proof structure; the scheme-theoretic argument
403	  above is the spelled-out form for the Lean body.
404	\end{proof}
405	
406	\section{Headline: genus-$0$ curve over $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$}
407	
408	\begin{theorem}
409	
410	\leanok
411	[Genus-$0$ curve over $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$]
412	  \label{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}
413	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
414	  \uses{def:genus, lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections,
415	        lem:degree_via_pole_divisor, lem:degree_one_morphism_iso,
416	        cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero,
417	        thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero,
418	        def:codim1_cycles, def:divisor_degree}
419	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S1 Example~1.3.5 (genus-zero classification;
420	  % "rational $\iff g = 0$" + "rational curve $\cong \mathbb P^1$ for
421	  % complete nonsingular curves") (read from
422	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314, doc p.~297).
423	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is \emph{rational}
424	  % if it is birational to $\mathbf P^1$ (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in this
425	  % chapter are complete and nonsingular by definition, a curve $X$ is
426	  % rational if and only if $X \cong \mathbf P^1$ (I, 6.12). Now using
427	  % (1.3) we can show that $X$ is rational if and only if $g = 0$. We
428	  % already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely
429	  % suppose given a curve $X$ of genus 0. Let $P, Q$ be two distinct
430	  % points of $X$ and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor $D = P - Q$.
431	  % Since $\deg(K - D) = -2$, using (1.3.3) above, we have $l(K - D) = 0$,
432	  % and so we find $l(D) = 1$. But $D$ is a divisor of degree 0, so by
433	  % (1.2) we have $D \sim 0$, in other words $P \sim Q$. But this implies
434	  % $X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
435	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
436	  Let $\bar k$ be an algebraically closed field, and let $C$ be a smooth
437	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over $\bar k$ with $g(C) = 0$.
438	  Then there exists an isomorphism of $\bar k$-schemes
439	  \[
440	    \varphi \colon C \stackrel{\sim}{\longrightarrow} \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}.
441	  \]
442	  Concretely, the Lean signature
443	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} (the existing pinned
444	  target at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290}) is
445	  closed by the proof below; the existing interface block
446	  \Cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} of
447	  \Cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} is its consumer-side restatement,
448	  pinning the same Lean declaration.
449	\end{theorem}
450	
451	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the
452	% statement block above (Hartshorne states the classification and its
453	% proof inside the single Example; the proof is the second half beginning
454	% "We already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ \ldots"). The present
455	% proof body restates Hartshorne's argument in the project's notation
456	% (linear-system construction $C \to \mathbb P^1$ via $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C
457	% ([P]))$, degree-$1$ identification, isomorphism).
458	\begin{proof}
459	  \uses{def:genus, lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections,
460	        lem:degree_via_pole_divisor, lem:degree_one_morphism_iso,
461	        cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero,
462	        thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero,
463	        def:codim1_cycles, def:divisor_degree}
464	  Over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$ the smooth proper
465	  geometrically irreducible curve $C$ has closed points, all of which are
466	  $\bar k$-rational (the residue field of a closed point of a curve over
467	  $\bar k$ is a finite extension of $\bar k$, hence is $\bar k$ itself by
468	  algebraic closedness). Fix one such $\bar k$-rational point $P \in C$;
469	  this is the marked point used to build the linear system below.
470	
471	  \emph{Step 1: non-constant function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))$.}
472	  By Hartshorne's Riemann--Roch in genus zero
473	  (\Cref{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}) applied to the
474	  effective divisor $[P]$ of degree $1$, we obtain
475	  $\ell([P]) = \deg([P]) + 1 = 2$. The constant section
476	  $1 \in \bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))$ accounts for a
477	  one-dimensional $\bar k$-subspace; the two-dimensional quotient supplies
478	  a non-constant section $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
479	  \bar k \cdot 1$. This is the corollary
480	  \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
481	  chapter, \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OCofP.tex}).
482	
483	  \emph{Step 2: morphism $C \to \mathbb P^1$ via the linear system $(1, f)$.}
484	  The pair $(1, f) \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))^2$ generates the
485	  invertible sheaf $\mathcal O_C([P])$ at every point of $C$: at points
486	  $Q \in C \setminus \{P\}$ the section $1$ does not vanish in
487	  $\mathcal O_C([P])_Q \cong \mathcal O_{C, Q}$, and at the marked point
488	  $P$ the section $f$ does not vanish in $\mathcal O_C([P])_P$ (because $f$
489	  has a pole of order exactly $1$ at $P$ in the rational-function
490	  picture: $f \in K(C) \setminus \bar k$ with $\div(f)_\infty = [P]$).
491	  Therefore by \Cref{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections} there
492	  exists a unique morphism
493	  \[
494	    \varphi \;:=\; \varphi_{(1, f)} \colon C \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
495	  \]
496	  with $\varphi^* x_0 = 1$ and $\varphi^* x_1 = f$ (so the pullback of
497	  the standard ratio coordinate $u = x_1 / x_0$ on $\mathbb P^1 \setminus
498	  \{\infty\}$ is $f$, and the pullback of the inverse coordinate
499	  $t_\infty = x_0 / x_1 = 1 / f$ is the local parameter $1/f$ at $P$).
500	
501	  \emph{Step 3: $\deg(\varphi) = 1$ via the pole divisor of $f$.} The
502	  morphism $\varphi$ is non-constant (since $f$ is non-constant as a
503	  rational function on $C$, the pullback $\varphi^* u = f$ takes
504	  infinitely many values, ruling out a constant $\varphi$). By
505	  \Cref{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} applied to $\varphi$,
506	  \[
507	    \deg(\varphi)
508	    \;=\;
509	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{Q \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
510	                  \ord_Q(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [Q]\,\right).
511	  \]
512	  We compute the pole divisor of $f$ as a rational function on $C$:
513	  since $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))$ with $f$ non-constant and
514	  $\mathcal O_C([P])$ the line bundle of the effective divisor $[P]$, the
515	  rational-function $f$ has pole divisor $(f)_\infty = [P]$ (the
516	  unique pole of order $1$ at $P$, no other poles; this is the standard
517	  identification of $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))$ with the rational functions
518	  $g \in K(C)$ satisfying $\div(g) + D \geq 0$, specialised to
519	  $D = [P]$). Hence $\varphi^{-1}(\infty) = \{P\}$ with multiplicity~$1$
520	  and
521	  \[
522	    \deg(\varphi) \;=\; \deg([P]) \;=\; 1.
523	  \]
524	
525	  \emph{Step 4: $\varphi$ is an isomorphism.} By Hartshorne's IV.2
526	  identification $\deg(\varphi) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]$, the
527	  degree-one conclusion of Step~3 rewrites as
528	  $\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C) = 1$ (with
529	  the canonical $\varphi$-induced
530	  \texttt{[Algebra \,(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField\,
531	   C.functionField]} instance). By
532	  \Cref{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} applied to the non-constant
533	  morphism $\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (smooth proper
534	  geometrically irreducible curves over $\bar k$ on both sides), under
535	  this function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis, $\varphi$ is an
536	  isomorphism of $\bar k$-schemes.
537	
538	  This produces the required isomorphism $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$
539	  closing the headline target
540	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}.
541	
542	  \emph{Comparison with Hartshorne's original argument.} Hartshorne's
543	  Example~IV.1.3.5 phrases the argument in terms of two distinct points
544	  $P, Q \in C$ and the degree-zero divisor $D = P - Q$, deriving $P \sim Q$
545	  and concluding via II.6.10.1 that $C$ is rational; combined with I.6.12
546	  this yields $C \cong \mathbb P^1$. The present proof restates the same
547	  combinatorial content via the more directly formalisable linear-system
548	  construction: instead of going through the $P \sim Q$ statement and
549	  invoking II.6.10.1 as a black box, we produce the explicit morphism
550	  $\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1$ from the two sections $(1, f)$ of
551	  $\mathcal O_C([P])$ given by Riemann--Roch and identify its degree
552	  directly. The two arguments are mathematically equivalent and have the
553	  same dependency footprint (Riemann--Roch in genus zero $\Rightarrow$
554	  $\ell([P]) = 2$ $\Rightarrow$ non-constant $f$ $\Rightarrow$ degree-$1$
555	  map to $\mathbb P^1$ $\Rightarrow$ iso); the linear-system phrasing maps
556	  more cleanly onto the Mathlib \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} API and
557	  the project's existing Weil-divisor/degree pipeline of \texttt{RR.1}.
558	\end{proof}
559
